preferences.snapToGridDescription: When enabled, components will snap to the grid as they move.
preferences.gridSpacingDescription: Controls the spacing between major grid lines (in metres).
preferences.signConventionDescription: Controls how signs are assigned to radii of curvature. The focal length is positive for converging optics in both conventions.
preferences.newCartesianDescription: Distances measured from the optic along the optical axis. Right is positive. Biconvex lens: R₁ > 0, R₂ < 0.
preferences.realIsPositiveDescription: Real (physical) distances are positive. Biconvex lens: R₁ > 0, R₂ > 0.
preferences.partialReflectionDescription: When disabled, glass surfaces transmit rays without producing Fresnel-reflected rays.
preferences.curvatureDisplayDescription: When enabled, sliders show curvature κ = 1/R (m⁻¹) instead of radius of curvature R (m).

adjust.removeSelectedElement: Press Delete or Backspace to remove the selected optical element (when a text field is not focused)
adjust.stepFocusedControl: Press + or - to increase or decrease the focused control

controls.toggleHelp: Press H to show or hide this keyboard help dialog

tools.toggleMeasuringTape: Press M to show or hide the measuring tape
tools.toggleProtractor: Press P to show or hide the protractor
tools.toggleExtendedRays: Press E to turn extended rays on or off
tools.toggleShowHandles: Press K to show or hide drag handles on optical elements
tools.toggleFocalMarkers: Press F to show or hide focal point markers
tools.toggleGrid: Press G to show or hide the grid
tools.toggleSnapToGrid: Press S to turn snap to grid on or off when the grid is visible

infoDialog.title: How to use this simulation
infoDialog.addElementsBody: Drag an optical element from the carousel on the left into the play area. Use the page dots to browse more components.
infoDialog.moveElementsBody: Drag the body of an element to reposition it. Drag handles adjust size, angle, curvature, and other geometry.
infoDialog.editElementsBody: Click an element to open the edit panel. Type numeric values or use sliders and steppers to change wavelength, focal length, index, and more.
infoDialog.removeElementsBody: Press Delete or Backspace, use the trash control in the edit panel, or drag the element back onto the carousel.
infoDialog.raysBody: Rays update continuously as you move optics. Use Ray Density in the Tools panel to show more or fewer sample rays.
infoDialog.toolsPanelBody: Open Tools (upper right) for ray density, grid, snap-to-grid, measuring tape, protractor, extended rays, handles, and focal markers.
infoDialog.measureBody: Turn on the measuring tape for distances in metres, or the protractor for angles. Both can be dragged on the play area.
infoDialog.extendedRaysBody: Toggle Extended Rays to show traced paths beyond surfaces (helpful for lenses and mirrors). Turn it off to emphasize outgoing rays only.
infoDialog.gridBody: Show the Grid for visual alignment. When the grid is on, Snap to Grid snaps dragged elements to grid intersections.
infoDialog.shortcutsBody: With no text field focused: M tape, P protractor, E extended rays, K handles, F focal points, G grid, S snap (when grid is on). Esc deselects.
